Structural System Summary

Primary system: Wood-framed structure with CMU shear walls and stair/elevator cores. Lateral system: CMU shear walls and wood shear walls. Floor system: Engineered wood I-joists with 3/4" T&G sheathing. Roof system: Pre-engineered wood trusses with 3/4" T&G sheathing. Foundation interface: Concrete shallow foundations (spread footings and wall footings). Special conditions: Very limited structural steel scope consisting of columns, beams, and lintels supporting wood framing. Outdoor trellis structure.
Approximate Tonnage Estimate

Primary structural steel: ~4.5 t Secondary / misc steel: ~0.8 t Combined project total: ~5.3 t Basis: "Counted from framing plan S1.02 and schedules S0.02. Tonnage is highly preliminary as all column and lintel lengths are missing and must be assumed or scaled."

12. READY-TO-SEND RFI PACKAGE
🔴 Critical RFIs (must answer before modeling): RFI-001 through RFI-002 --- RFI-001 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02 — Steel Column Schedule Priority: Critical Blocking: Yes

Question: The Steel Column Schedule on sheet S0.02 does not list the lengths for column marks C5, C6, and C8B. Please provide a complete schedule with all required column lengths (or top/bottom of steel elevations) to allow for material procurement and modeling.
Expected Response Format: A revised Steel Column Schedule on sheet S0.02 or a supplementary schedule providing all column lengths.
RFI-002 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02, S1.01 — Anchor Bolt Details for Main Columns Priority: Critical Blocking: Yes

Question: The Steel Column Schedule on S0.02 and foundation details do not specify the required embedment depth or above-concrete projection for the anchor bolts on columns C5, C6, and C8B. Please provide these critical dimensions.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation of dimensions or a detail showing required embedment and projection for each column type.
--- 🟠 Urgent RFIs (answer within first week): RFI-003 through RFI-004 --- RFI-003 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02 — Lintel Schedules Priority: Urgent Blocking: Yes

Question: The Lintel Schedule and Brick Veneer Loose Lintel Schedule on sheet S0.02 do not provide lengths for any of the steel lintels (e.g., L1, L2, L7x4x3/8). The schedules refer to opening sizes on Architectural drawings which were not included in the bid package. Please provide all steel lintel lengths or the relevant architectural drawings.
Expected Response Format: A complete lintel schedule with lengths, or issuance of the corresponding architectural elevation/plan sheets.
RFI-004 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.01 — General Structural Note #12 Priority: Urgent Blocking: Yes

Question: General Structural Note #12 delegates the design of steel connections to the fabricator. Please confirm the required submittal package for this delegated design (e.g., sealed calculations, erection drawings with connection details). Additionally, please provide any specific connection load tables, criteria, or special considerations that must be used for the design.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation of delegated design submittal requirements and provision of any necessary design criteria or load tables.
--- 🟡 Standard RFIs (answer before drawing release): RFI-005 through RFI-007 --- RFI-005 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers; rba. Re: S1.02 — Canopy Scope Priority: Standard Blocking: No

Question: The Second Floor Framing Plan (S1.02) indicates a "PRE-FABRICATED CANOPY BY OTHERS". Please clarify if the structural steel fabricator's scope includes any support beams, columns, embeds, or connection hardware associated with this canopy.
Expected Response Format: Written clarification of the steel fabrication scope related to the canopy.
RFI-006 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02 — Base Plate Grout Thickness Priority: Standard Blocking: No

Question: The base plate details shown in the Steel Column Schedule (S0.02) do not specify a grout thickness. Please provide the required grout thickness to be used under all steel column base plates.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation of the required grout thickness.
RFI-007 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02, S1.02 — Non-Standard HSS Sizes Priority: Standard Blocking: No

Question: The drawings call for several HSS sizes (HSS18x6x3/8, HSS9x5x3/8, HSS6x6x5/16, HSS8x6x3/8) that are not common production sizes per AISC. Please confirm these sections are available or provide preferred substitute sections that are readily available.
Expected Response Format: Confirmation of availability or a list of acceptable substitute sections.
